page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.3xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance designed for high-capacity local storage and memory-intensive workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 12 dedicated vCPUs, consisting of 6 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The processor is equipped with 320 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix and vector operations.

The memory subsystem consists of 96.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 6 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance is bundled with 7500 GB of NVMe SSD storage, delivering substantial capacity for data-intensive operations. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark results show top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 34.64 GB/sec, Geekbench single-core scoring at 2307.0, and Geekbench text processing at 2928.0. Memory performance is strong, with a Passmark memory score of 2807.39 and read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 1033.5 GB/sec. Multi-core performance remains in the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 11511.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 13184.81. The instance also demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models at 382.91 tokens/sec.

Given its hardware profile, the i7ie.3xlarge offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring dense local storage paired with high memory capacity. It is well-suited for high-throughput database operations, data warehousing, large-scale caching layers, and localized data processing tasks that benefit from fast NVMe storage and DDR5 memory speeds.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.3xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 3.6 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. The instance exhibits top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and gzip compression benchmarks, alongside strong memory bandwidth. With its high storage-to-compute ratio and substantial memory allocation, this instance is designed for demanding database systems, data warehousing, and high-throughput local data processing workloads.

tagline: A storage-optimized platform combining high-capacity local NVMe SSDs with DDR5 memory for data-intensive and database workloads.

96.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 8.0 GB per core
7500 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
